4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
99	
100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese
131	
132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
140	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
141	
142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
177	of genus `0` over `k`. The underlying scheme is `Spec k` (the terminal object), with
178	the trivial group structure, smoothness of relative dimension `0` (matching the
179	hypothesis `genus C = 0`), properness, and geometric irreducibility. The
180	`isAlbaneseFor` field encodes the universal property: for every $k$-rational
181	marked point `P` of `C`, the universal morphism `C ⟶ Spec k` is the unique
182	terminal map `toUnit C`; the substantive content is the rigidity statement
183	`rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`), gated on the
184	shared cotangent-vanishing pile (iter-129+).
185	
186	**Status**: iter-127 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is iter-138+
187	work, after pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land.
188	
189	**Iter-135**: now consumed by the genus-stratified body of
190	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Change 2.1 of iter-135 refactor); body
191	closure remains iter-138+ work but the witness is now load-bearing
192	for the protected `nonempty_jacobianWitness` chain. -/
193	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
194	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
195	    (h : genus C = 0) :
196	    JacobianWitness C :=
197	  sorry
198	
199	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
200	of positive genus `g ≥ 1` over `k`. The underlying scheme is the Albanese
201	variety of `C` — classically the connected component of the identity of
202	`Pic_{C/k}`, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel–Jacobi
203	morphism on `Sym^g(C)`. Both constructions require infrastructure not yet
204	in Mathlib (FGA representability of the Picard functor for Route A;
205	symmetric powers + finite-group quotients + Stein factorisation +
206	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch for Route B); the iter-123 M3 route audit
207	(`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`) records both at midpoint ~6500 and
208	~9000 LOC respectively.
209	
210	**Status**: iter-134 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is M3
211	work, currently OFF-CRITICAL-PATH until M2 closes (per STRATEGY.md
212	§ M3, user-escalation-pending).
213	
214	**Iter-135**: the genus-stratified body restructure of
215	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is now in place (Change 2.1 of iter-135
216	refactor); this scaffold is the positive-genus arm. Body closure
217	remains M3 work (off-critical-path; user-escalation-pending per
218	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). -/
219	noncomputable def positiveGenusWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
220	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
221	    (hg : 0 < genus C) :
222	    JacobianWitness C :=
223	  sorry
224	
225	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
226	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
227	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
228	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
229	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
230	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
231	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
232	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
233	field of `JacobianWitness`).
234	
235	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
236	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
237	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
238	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
239	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
240	existence is assumed.
241	
242	**Iter-135 body restructure**: the body is now a `by_cases h :
243	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)
244	and `positiveGenusWitness` (positive-genus arm). Both arms' bodies
245	remain `sorry` (M2.b body closure iter-138+; M3 body closure
246	off-critical-path); the restructure converts the inline `sorry` here
247	into honest delegation to the two scaffolds, removing one inline-`sorry`
248	site without prejudging either arm's body closure. -/
249	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
250	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
251	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
252	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
253	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
254	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩
255	
256	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
257	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
258	on it. -/
259	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
260	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
261	    JacobianWitness C :=
262	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
263	
264	-- data
265	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
266	
267	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
268	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
269	witness is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C scaffolding
270	(the other being `genusZeroWitness`), both scheduled for body closure post
271	M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the
272	witness — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of
273	relative dimension `0` is `Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is
274	absorbed into `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
275	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
276	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
277	  (jacobianWitness C).J
278	
279	namespace Jacobian
280	
281	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
282	
283	-- data
284	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
285	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
286	
287	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
288	genus of `C`. -/
289	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
290	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
291	
292	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
293	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
294	
295	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
296	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
297	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
298	
299	end Jacobian
300	
301	end AlgebraicGeometry
302

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Sheaf condition along the structure-sheaf forget composite
10	
11	Phase A step 1 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the `HasSheafCompose` instance for the
12	forget composite `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` on the Grothendieck
13	topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space. This is the gateway to
14	computing `H¹(C, O_C)` as an abelian group (and ultimately as a `k`-vector
15	space for the genus).
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex`.
18	
19	## Status (closed iteration 003)
20	
21	The single instance below is honestly closed (5-line body via
22	`Limits.comp_preservesLimits` + `hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize`).
23	Phase A step 1 of `STRATEGY.md`.
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	
28	universe u
29	
30	open CategoryTheory Limits
31	
32	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
33	
34	/-- The forget composite `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` preserves the
35	sheaf condition on the Grothendieck topology of opens of any topological space.
36	Equivalently: a sheaf of commutative rings on a topological space is in
37	particular a sheaf of abelian groups (post-composing with the forgetful is
38	sound). -/
39	instance instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
40	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).HasSheafCompose
41	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
42	       CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
43	  haveI : PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u}
44	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
45	        CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
46	    Limits.comp_preservesLimits _ _
47	  CategoryTheory.hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize _
48	
49	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
50

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.SheafCompose
7	
8	/-!
9	# Structure sheaf as a sheaf of abelian groups, sheafification and Ext
10	
11	Phase A steps 2–4 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the `HasSheafify` and `HasExt`
12	instances on the topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space (valued
13	in `AddCommGrpCat`), together with the structure sheaf of a scheme viewed as
14	a sheaf of abelian groups via the iter-004 `HasSheafCompose` instance.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (closed iteration 004)
19	
20	All three declarations are honestly closed by the iter-004 multilane prover
21	round (lanes anthropic + deepseek; merged commit `2686f4e`).
22	-/
23	
24	set_option autoImplicit false
25	
26	universe u
27	
28	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
29	
30	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
31	
32	/-- Phase A step 2: sheafification on the topology of opens of any
33	topological space, valued in `AddCommGrpCat`. -/
34	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
35	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
36	      AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
37	  inferInstance
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 3: `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe annotation
40	`HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
41	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}`; other annotations produce a
42	`HasExt.{max ?u_1 ?u_2, ?u_2, ?u_1}` mismatch. -/
43	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
44	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
45	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
46	        AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
47	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
48	
49	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
50	
51	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
52	
53	/-- Phase A step 4 first half: the structure sheaf of a scheme, viewed as a
54	sheaf of abelian groups via the iter-004 `HasSheafCompose` instance for the
55	forget composite `CommRingCat → RingCat → AddCommGrpCat`. -/
56	noncomputable def toAbSheaf (C : Scheme.{u}) :
57	    CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
58	      AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
59	  (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
60	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
61	      CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj C.sheaf
62	
63	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: sheafification, Ext, and the structure sheaf
10	
11	Phase A step 5 (per `STRATEGY.md`):
12	
13	* **Prerequisites** (iter-005, closed): the `HasSheafify` and `HasExt` instances
14	  on the topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space, valued in
15	  `ModuleCat k`. The `Linear k` enrichment on the resulting sheaf category is
16	  auto-inferable from Mathlib and therefore needs no scaffold here; together
17	  with `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule` it gives the path to a
18	  `Module k` structure on `Ext` groups.
19	* **Main** (iter-006, scaffolded here): the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme
20	  `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` viewed as a sheaf of `k`-modules. Built
21	  from the structure-morphism-derived ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` (helpers (1)–(5))
22	  and packaged as the presheaf `toModuleKPresheaf` (6), proven to be a sheaf
23	  by `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (7), and bundled as `toModuleKSheaf` (8).
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
26	
27	## Status
28	
29	All Phase A step 5 declarations are closed: this file builds the
30	`ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf `toModuleKSheaf C` for
31	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` from helpers (1)–(5), packages the presheaf
32	`toModuleKPresheaf` (6), proves it is a sheaf (7), and bundles it as
33	`toModuleKSheaf` (8). The construction is downstream from
34	`Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` and `Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`.
35	
36	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
37	
38	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
39	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
40	  lives at iter-051).
41	* H⁰ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
42	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
43	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
44	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
45	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
46	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding — $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
47	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
48	  removed from this file.
49	-/
50	
51	set_option autoImplicit false
52	
53	universe u v
54	
55	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
56	
57	namespace CategoryTheory
58	
59	universe u₂ v₂
60	
61	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
62	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
63	`ext; rfl`. -/
64	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
65	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
66	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
67	
68	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
69	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
70	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
71	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
72	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
73	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
74	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
75	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
76	
77	namespace Adjunction
78	
79	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
80	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
81	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
82	
83	include adj
84	
85	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
86	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
87	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
88	default simp lemmas. -/
89	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
90	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
91	    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])
92	
93	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
94	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
95	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
96	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
97	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
98	
99	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
100	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
101	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
102	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
103	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
104	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
105	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
106	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
107	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
108	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
109	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
110	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
111	      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }
112	
113	end Adjunction
114	
115	end CategoryTheory
116	
117	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
118	
119	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
120	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
121	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
122	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
123	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
124	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
125	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
126	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
127	  inferInstance
128	
129	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
130	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
131	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
132	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
133	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
134	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
135	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
136	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
137	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
138	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
139	
140	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
141	
142	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
143	
144	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
145	
146	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
147	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
148	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
149	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
150	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
151	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
152	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
153	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
154	
155	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
156	map of `kToSection`. -/
157	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
158	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
159	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
160	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
161	
162	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
163	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
164	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
165	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
166	  rfl
167	
168	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
169	natural in `U`. -/
170	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
171	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
172	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
173	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
174	  rfl
175	
176	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
177	`algebraMap`. -/
178	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
179	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
180	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
181	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
182	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
183	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
184	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
185	
186	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
187	
188	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
189	
190	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
191	
192	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
193	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
194	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
195	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
196	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
197	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
198	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
199	      map_add' := fun x y => by
200	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
201	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
202	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
203	        congr 1
204	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
205	          (C := C) f r }
206	  map_id U := by
207	    ext x
208	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
209	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
210	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
211	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
212	    ext x
213	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
214	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
215	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x

271	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
272	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any

274	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
275	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
276	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
277	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
278	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
279	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
280	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
281	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
282	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
283	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
284	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
285	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
286	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
287	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀

289	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored

291	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
292	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
293	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
294	
295	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
296	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
297	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
298	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
299	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
300	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).

302	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
303	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
304	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
305	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
306	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`

308	iter-015+. -/

310	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}

312	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
313	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
314	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
315	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n

317	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,

319	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
320	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
321	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
322	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
323	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
324	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's

329	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/

333	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]

335	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
336	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
337	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
338	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
339	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀

341	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
342	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
343	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
344	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
345	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
346	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
347	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
348	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
349	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
350	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
351	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/

353	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

356	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))

358	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
359	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm

361	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
362	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
363	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H⁰ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]

365	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
366	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
367	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in

369	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
370	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
371	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}

373	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
374	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
375	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj

377	    Module.Finite k (HModule' k F 0 X) :=

380	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero` to
381	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`. The
382	Grothendieck topology `Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat` is auto-inferred

396	        (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :

400	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule'_zero` to
401	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`,
402	evaluated at an arbitrary open `U` of the underlying topological space. Parallel
403	of `module_finite_HModule_zero_curve` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised
404	carrier `HModule'`.  Same auto-inferred topology / instance setup. The open `U`
405	is an explicit parameter (unlike the implicit topology); the sheaf argument is
406	inferred. Used downstream in the cover-evaluation chain for proper geometrically

410	morally `Module.Finite k (Γ(X_i, O_C))` for affine $X_i$ — to the H⁰ cohomology

413	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

416	    [Module.Finite k
417	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
418	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶

423	/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
424	geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
425	every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
426	is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
427	a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object — see iter-014 abbrev). The class
428	is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
429	producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
430	project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
431	scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
432	class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
433	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
434	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
435	    Prop where
436	  subsingleton_HModule' : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
437	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U → ∀ i, 0 < i →
438	      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)
439	
440	/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
441	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
442	`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
443	the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
444	Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M → Module.Finite R M` instance (any
445	subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
446	This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
447	with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
448	corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
449	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
450	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
451	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
452	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
453	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
454	    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
455	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
456	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
457	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
458	  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
459	    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
460	  inferInstance
461	
462	/-- Iter-043 wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the
463	algebraic statement that the global Hom group
464	`((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` is finite over `k`. Morally
465	`Γ(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`) being finite over `k`. On a
466	proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with $F = O_C$, this is
467	$\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper geometrically
468	connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance from the
469	geometric content of $C$ (supplied at iter-046).
470	
471	**Historical note on the abandoned per-affine-open variant.** An earlier
472	iter-041 attempt packaged Hom-finiteness of
473	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free k).obj U) ⟶ F)` **for every
474	affine open** `U`. By Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify adjunctions this
475	Hom group is `≃ₗ[k] Γ(U, F)`, and on a proper smooth $k$-curve $C$ with
476	$F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a proper
477	affine open — e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
478	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
479	infinite over $k$. That per-open class therefore admits no producer
480	instance on a non-trivial proper curve and has been deleted as dead
481	scaffolding.
482	
483	This wholespace version captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
484	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
485	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
486	vanishing). -/
487	class IsHModuleHomFinite
488	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
489	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
490	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
491	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
492	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)
493	
494	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness class
495	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
496	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H⁰ algebraic bridge
497	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ⟶ F)`)
498	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Works at the global level
499	(one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
500	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
501	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
502	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
503	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
504	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
505	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
506	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F
507	
508	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
509	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
510	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
511	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
512	patterns. -/
513	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
514	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
515	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
516	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
517	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
518	
519	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H⁰
520	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
521	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
522	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
523	
524	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
525	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
526	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
527	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
528	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
529	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
530	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
531	
532	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
533	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (where the algebra
534	structure on `Γ(C, ⊤)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
535	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
536	transport the base ring from intermediate `Γ(Spec k, ⊤)` to `k` along
537	the ring iso `Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
538	of algebra maps reduces to showing
539	`kToSection C (op ⊤).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
540	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `⊤ ⟶ ⊤` hom-set +
541	`Functor.map_id`.
542	
543	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
544	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
545	`constantSheafΓAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
546	`Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
547	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
548	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
549	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
550	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
551	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
552	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
553	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
554	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
555	               (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
556	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
557	  have hM_inter :
558	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
559	        (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
560	    rw [← RingHom.finite_algebraMap]; exact hf
561	  refine Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv
562	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
563	    (RingEquiv.refl _) ?_
564	  ext x
565	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, RingEquiv.refl_apply,
566	    Function.comp_apply, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
567	  have h_kts : (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom =
568	                (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) := by
569	    ext y
570	    simp only [Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
571	      RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
572	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id (Opposite.op (⊤ :
573	                TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))) _
574	  calc (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)))
575	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x)
576	       = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom)
577	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x) :=
578	              congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h_kts) _
579	    _  = C.hom.appTop.hom x := by
580	        simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
581	        congr 1
582	        change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).hom ≫
583	              (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv).hom x = x
584	        rw [Iso.hom_inv_id]; rfl
585	
586	/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
587	`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
588	of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.
589	
590	The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
591	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
592	`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
593	the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
594	`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `⊤` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
595	for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
596	converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
597	upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).
598	
599	Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
600	/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
601	Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `≃ₗ[k]`) to construct the producer
602	instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
603	noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
604	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
605	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
606	    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
607	      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
608	  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
609	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
610	
611	/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
612	`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
613	yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Γ).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
614	integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.
615	
616	The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
617	auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
618	declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`,
619	but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
620	`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` — these are *the same Module* (via the
621	iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
622	register the typeclass under the new spelling.
623	
624	This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
625	bridging from `Sheaf.Γ.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ⟶ -)`
626	requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction, which is multi-iteration
627	project-local infrastructure. -/
628	theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
629	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
630	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
631	    Module.Finite k
632	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
633	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
634	  haveI : Module.Finite k
635	      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
636	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
637	    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
638	  exact Module.Finite.equiv
639	    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
640	
641	/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction. For any
642	sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
643	between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ⟶ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
644	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
645	global sections of `F`).
646	
647	Built from `(constantSheafΓAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
648	The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
649	the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
650	`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Γ` Additive +
651	Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafΓAdj`).
652	
653	Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
654	to bridge from `Sheaf.Γ.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
655	finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
656	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
657	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
658	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
659	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
660	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
661	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
662	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
663	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
664	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
665	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
666	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
667	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
668	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
669	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
670	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
671	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
672	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
673	
674	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
675	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
676	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
677	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
678	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
679	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
680	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
681	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
682	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
683	
684	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
685	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
686	
687	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
688	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
689	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
690	      as `k`-modules.
691	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
692	      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
693	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
694	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
695	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
696	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
697	
698	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
699	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
700	closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
701	
702	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
703	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
704	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
705	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
706	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
707	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
708	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
709	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
710	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
711	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
712	  module_finite_hom := by
713	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
714	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
715	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
716	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
717	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
718	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
719	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
720	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
721	
722	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
723	
724	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
725	
726	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
727	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
728	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
729	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
730	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
731	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
732	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
733	
734	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
735	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
736	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
737	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
738	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
739	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
740	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
741	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
742	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
743	
744	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
745	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
746	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
747	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
748	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
749	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
750	acyclic. -/
751	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
752	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
753	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
754	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
755	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
756	
757	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
758	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
759	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
760	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
761	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
762	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
763	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
764	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
765	
766	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
767	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
768	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
769	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
770	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
771	generality. -/
772	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
773	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
774	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
775	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
776	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
777	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
778	
779	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
780	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
781	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
782	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
783	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
784	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
785	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
786	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
787	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
788	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
789	
790	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
791	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
792	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
793	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
794	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
795	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
796	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
797	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
798	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
799	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
800	  rfl
801	
802	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
803	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
804	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
805	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
806	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
807	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
808	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
809	  rfl
810	
811	/-- Iter-048: Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `𝒰` is
812	*Čech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree Čech
813	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense — `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n`
814	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
815	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
816	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
817	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
818	
819	This is the foundational Čech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
820	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
821	with the iter-049+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
822	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
823	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
824	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
825	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
826	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
827	    ∀ (n : ℕ), 0 < n → Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)
828	
829	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via Čech acyclicity + comparison.
830	
831	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` AND an explicit
832	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i)`
833	(the Čech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
834	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i))` for `n ≥ 1`.
835	
836	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
837	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
838	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the Čech-side combinatorial
839	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
840	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
841	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
842	
843	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
844	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
845	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
846	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
847	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
848	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
849	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]
850	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
851	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
852	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
853	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
854	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by
855	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
856	    (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
857	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
858	
859	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
860	
861	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
862	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
863	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
864	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
865	producer instance. -/
866	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
867	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
868	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
869	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
870	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
871	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
872	        Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
873	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
874	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
875	  Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover (𝒰 := 𝒰) compIso n hn
876	
877	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Mayer-Vietoris LES core for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology
11	
12	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 1) contains the MV LES core for
13	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`-valued cohomology: the Mathlib gap-fill
